perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 2 STANDSTILL Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 2 STANDSTILL Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu. However, Zulu may engage in preliminary discussions with potential alternative acquirers, provided that no definitive agreement is reached before October 31, 2007.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds a clause allowing Zulu to engage in preliminary discussions with potential acquirers, which directly contradicts the standstill agreement that prevents them from soliciting or encouraging such proposals. This creates an in-text contradiction within Article 2, making the standstill obligation unclear and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 2 STANDSTILL Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu. In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing Zulu to engage in preliminary discussions with potential acquirers, while the contradicted text, which is the same as the original text, states that Zulu and its shareholders will not solicit or encourage submissions of proposals from any third party. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. Zulu cannot both engage in preliminary discussions (changed text) and not solicit or encourage proposals (contradicted text).

perturbation.original_text: In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals. However, Zulu is not required to notify Swansi of unsolicited offers.
perturbation.explanation: The added text creates a contradiction by stating that Zulu is not required to notify Swansi of unsolicited offers, directly conflicting with the previous sentence that mandates notification of *any* solicitation, proposal, or offer. This creates uncertainty about Zulu's notification obligations and weakens the standstill agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals. In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals. However, Zulu is not required to notify Swansi of unsolicited offers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception stating that Zulu is not required to notify Swansi of *unsolicited* offers. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text which states that Zulu *shall* provide written notice to Swansi of *any* solicitation, proposal, or offer. The added text creates a clear inconsistency about Zulu's notification obligations, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 5 DEFINITIVE AGREEMENTS; DISCLOSURE The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by October 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 5 DEFINITIVE AGREEMENTS; DISCLOSURE The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by October 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will continue in full force and effect indefinitely, unless terminated by mutual written consent of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the consequence of failing to enter into a definitive agreement by October 31, 2007. Originally, the agreement would have limited force after that date. The altered text states that the agreement continues indefinitely unless terminated by mutual consent, directly contradicting the original terms and creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration and termination conditions. This contradicts Article 2 as the Standstill is till October 31, 2007.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 2 STANDSTILL Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu. In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that the agreement would have limited force after October 31, 2007, except for specific articles. The changed text states that the agreement continues indefinitely unless terminated by mutual consent. This directly contradicts Article 2, which defines the 'Term of this Agreement' including the standstill provision as ending on October 31, 2007. The change introduces an inconsistency as the standstill would have been terminated previously, but under the changed text, the agreement would still be in full effect beyond that date. Therefore, there is a direct conflict regarding the agreement's duration and termination conditions, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 4 INJUNCTION The parties hereto agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that in addition to other remedies, each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, and in such circumstances the offending party agrees to waive posting of a bond to secure any such equitable relief hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 4 INJUNCTION The parties hereto agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that in addition to other remedies, each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, except in cases where the breach is deemed minor, in which case only money damages will be available. The offending party agrees to waive posting of a bond to secure any such equitable relief hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except in cases where the breach is deemed minor, in which case only money damages will be available' creates ambiguity and contradiction within Article 4. It introduces the subjective term 'minor breach' without defining it, contradicting the initial assertion that injunctive relief is available for *any* breach. This adds an exception to the provision for injunctive relief which creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about when injunctive relief is actually available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 4 INJUNCTION The parties hereto agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that in addition to other remedies, each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, and in such circumstances the offending party agrees to waive posting of a bond to secure any such equitable relief hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the availability of injunctive relief ('except in cases where the breach is deemed minor, in which case only money damages will be available'), which directly contradicts the original assertion that 'each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief' for *any* breach. The original text does not allow for exceptions based on the severity of the breach, thus creating a direct in-text contradiction as per the definition of inconsistencies - in text contradiction.
